contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरघुराजपदाब्जनिकेतन पङ्कजलोचन मङ्गलराशे
चण्डमहाभुजदण्ड सुरारिविखण्डनपण्डित पाहि दयालो ।
पातकिनं च समुद्धर मां महतां हि सतामपि मानमुदारं
त्वां भजतो मम देहि दयाघन हे हनुमन् स्वपदाम्बुजदास्यम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīraghurājapadābjanikētana paṅkajalōchana maṅgaḻarāśē
chaṇḍamahābhujadaṇḍa surārivikhaṇḍanapaṇḍita pāhi dayāḻō ।
pātakinaṃ cha samuddhara māṃ mahatāṃ hi satāmapi mānamudāraṃ
tvāṃ bhajatō mama dēhi dayāghana hē hanuman svapadāmbujadāsyam ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: You who dwell at the lotus feet of Śrī Raghu-rāja, O lotus-eyed treasury of all auspiciousness, O wise one with fierce mighty arms that shatter the enemy-gods, O compassionate one — lift up this sinner. For the honor of the great and the virtuous is ever magnanimous. O Hanumān, cloud of compassion, grant me servitude at your lotus feet, to me who worship you. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
संसृतितापमहानलदग्धतनूरुहमर्मतनोरतिवेलं
पुत्रधनस्वजनात्मगृहादिषु सक्तमतेरतिकिल्बिषमूर्तेः ।
केनचिदप्यमलेन पुराकृतपुण्यसुपुञ्जलवेन विभो वै
त्वां भजतो मम देहि दयाघन हे हनुमन् स्वपदाम्बुजदास्यम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṃsṛtitāpamahānaladagdhatanūruhamarmatanōrativēlaṃ
putradhanasvajanātmagṛhādiṣu saktamatēratikilbiṣamūrtēḥ ।
kēnachidapyamalēna purākṛtapuṇyasupuñjalavēna vibhō vai
tvāṃ bhajatō mama dēhi dayāghana hē hanuman svapadāmbujadāsyam ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: My body's hairs are scorched beyond endurance by the great fire of worldly suffering; my mind is attached to sons, wealth, kinsmen, body, home and the like — ensnared in its sinful form. Yet by some tiny spark of merit accumulated in the past by some holy being — O Lord, O Hanumān, cloud of compassion, grant me servitude at your lotus feet, to me who worship you. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
संसृतिकूपमनल्पमघोरनिदाघनिदानमजस्रमशेषं
प्राप्य सुदुःखसहस्रभुजङ्गविषैकसमाकुलसर्वतनोर्मे ।
घोरमहाकृपणापदमेव गतस्य हरे पतितस्य भवाब्धौ
त्वां भजतो मम देहि दयाघन हे हनुमन् स्वपदाम्बुजदास्यम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṃsṛtikūpamanalpamaghōranidāghanidānamajasramaśēṣaṃ
prāpya suduḥkhasahasrabhujaṅgaviṣaikasamākulasarvatanōrmē ।
ghōramahākṛpaṇāpadamēva gatasya harē patitasya bhavābdhau
tvāṃ bhajatō mama dēhi dayāghana hē hanuman svapadāmbujadāsyam ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I have fallen into the fathomless, terrible, incessant well of worldly existence — the boundless root of all affliction — and my entire body writhes with the venom of a thousand-armed serpent of acute sorrow. A miserable wretch gone to ruin, fallen into the ocean of rebirth — O Hanumān, cloud of compassion, grant me servitude at your lotus feet, to me who worship you. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
संसृतिसिन्धुविशालकरालमहाबलकालझषग्रसनार्तं
व्यग्रसमग्रधियं कृपणं च महामदनक्रसुचक्रहृतासुम् ।
कालमहारसनोर्मिनिपीडितमुद्धर दीनमनन्यगतिं मां
त्वां भजतो मम देहि दयाघन हे हनुमन् स्वपदाम्बुजदास्यम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṃsṛtisindhuviśālakarālamahābalakālajhaṣagrasanārtaṃ
vyagrasamagradhiyaṃ kṛpaṇaṃ cha mahāmadanakrasuchakrahṛtāsum ।
kālamahārasanōrminipīḍitamuddhara dīnamananyagatiṃ māṃ
tvāṃ bhajatō mama dēhi dayāghana hē hanuman svapadāmbujadāsyam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I am tormented by the great fierce fish of Time in the vast and terrifying ocean of worldly existence, my mind distracted and distressed; the mighty whirlpool of passion has robbed me of my very life-breath. O Hari, lift me up — wretched, with no other refuge, oppressed by the crushing waves of all-consuming Time — O Hanumān, cloud of compassion, grant me servitude at your lotus feet, to me who worship you. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
संसृतिघोरमहागहने चरतो मणिरञ्जितपुण्यसुमूर्तेः
मन्मथभीकरघोरमहोग्रमृगप्रवरार्दितगात्रसुसन्धेः ।
मत्सरतापविशेषनिपीडितबाह्यमतेश्च कथं चिदमेयं
त्वां भजतो मम देहि दयाघन हे हनुमन् स्वपदाम्बुजदास्यम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṃsṛtighōramahāgahanē charatō maṇirañjitapuṇyasumūrtēḥ
manmathabhīkaraghōramahōgramṛgapravarārditagātrasusandhēḥ ।
matsaratāpaviśēṣanipīḍitabāhyamatēścha kathaṃ chidamēyaṃ
tvāṃ bhajatō mama dēhi dayāghana hē hanuman svapadāmbujadāsyam ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Wandering in this terrifying dense forest of worldly existence, my body adorned with gems of virtue yet torn asunder by the ferocious beast of desire — my outward mind tormented by the burning affliction of envy — O Hanumān, cloud of compassion, grant me servitude at your lotus feet, to me who worship you. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
संसृतिवृक्षमनेकशताघनिदानमनन्तविकर्मसुशाखं
दुःखफलं करणादिपलाशमनङ्गसुपुष्पमचिन्त्यसुमूलम् ।
तं ह्यधिरुह्य हरे पतितं शरणागतमेव विमोचय मूढं
त्वां भजतो मम देहि दयाघन हे हनुमन् स्वपदाम्बुजदास्यम् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṃsṛtivṛkṣamanēkaśatāghanidānamanantavikarmasuśākhaṃ
duḥkhaphalaṃ karaṇādipalāśamanaṅgasupuṣpamachintyasumūlam ।
taṃ hyadhiruhya harē patitaṃ śaraṇāgatamēva vimōchaya mūḍhaṃ
tvāṃ bhajatō mama dēhi dayāghana hē hanuman svapadāmbujadāsyam ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The tree of worldly existence has hundreds of roots of countless sins, infinite branches of evil deeds, fruit of suffering, leaves of the senses, blossoms of passion — its root inconceivable. Climbing this tree, I fell, O Hari — deliver me who have come to you as refuge, a fool who has fallen. O Hanumān, cloud of compassion, grant me servitude at your lotus feet, to me who worship you. (6)

contentBlocks.original:
संसृतिपन्नगवक्त्रभयङ्करदंष्ट्रमहाविषदग्धशरीरं
प्राणविनिर्गमभीतिसमाकुलमन्दमनाथमतीव विषण्णम् ।
मोहमहाकुहरे पतितं दययोद्धर मामजितेन्द्रियकामं
त्वां भजतो मम देहि दयाघन हे हनुमन् स्वपदाम्बुजदास्यम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṃsṛtipannagavaktrabhayaṅkaradaṃṣṭramahāviṣadagdhaśarīraṃ
prāṇavinirgamabhītisamākulamandamanāthamatīva viṣaṇṇam ।
mōhamahākuharē patitaṃ dayayōddhara māmajitēndriyakāmaṃ
tvāṃ bhajatō mama dēhi dayāghana hē hanuman svapadāmbujadāsyam ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: My body scorched by the terrible poison of the serpent of worldly existence, filled with fear as life departs, utterly slothful, masterless, and deeply despondent — fallen into the great cave of delusion, a slave to ungoverned senses and desire — O Hanumān, with your compassion lift me up. O cloud of compassion, grant me servitude at your lotus feet, to me who worship you. (7)

contentBlocks.original:
इन्द्रियनामकचोरगणैर्हृततत्त्वविवेकमहाधनराशिं
संसृतिजालनिपातितमेव महाबलिभिश्च विखण्डितकायम् ।
त्वत्पदपद्ममनुत्तममाश्रितमाशु कपीश्वर पाहि कृपालो
त्वां भजतो मम देहि दयाघन हे हनुमन् स्वपदाम्बुजदास्यम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
indriyanāmakachōragaṇairhṛtatattvavivēkamahādhanarāśiṃ
saṃsṛtijālanipātitamēva mahābalibhiścha vikhaṇḍitakāyam ।
tvatpadapadmamanuttamamāśritamāśu kapīśvara pāhi kṛpāḻō
tvāṃ bhajatō mama dēhi dayāghana hē hanuman svapadāmbujadāsyam ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The great wealth of discriminating knowledge has been stolen by the thieving band of the senses; I have been trapped in the net of worldly existence, my body torn apart by the mighty ones. I who have taken sole refuge at your matchless lotus feet — save me quickly, O Lord of monkeys, O compassionate one! O Hanumān, cloud of compassion, grant me servitude at your lotus feet, to me who worship you. (8)

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्ममरुद्गणरुद्रमहेन्द्रकिरीटसुकोटिलसत्पदपीठं
दाशरथिं जपति क्षितिमण्डल एष निधाय सदैव हृदब्जे ।
तस्य हनूमत एव शिवङ्करमष्टकमेतदनिष्टहरं वै
यः सततं हि पठेत्स नरो लभतेऽच्युतरामपदाब्जनिवासम् ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmamarudgaṇarudramahēndrakirīṭasukōṭilasatpadapīṭhaṃ
dāśarathiṃ japati kṣitimaṇḍala ēṣa nidhāya sadaiva hṛdabjē ।
tasya hanūmata ēva śivaṅkaramaṣṭakamētadaniṣṭaharaṃ vai
yaḥ satataṃ hi paṭhētsa narō labhatē'chyutarāmapadābjanivāsam ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Brahmā, the Maruts, Rudra, Mahendra — their gem-studded diadems bow at the footstool of the son of Daśaratha (Rāma), whom all this earth-realm eternally enshrines in the lotus of the heart while ever repeating his name. This auspicious eightfold hymn of Hanumān, remover of all evil — whosoever recites it always shall attain dwelling at the lotus feet of Achyuta-Rāma. (9)
Thus ends the Śrī Hanumad-aṣṭakam composed by Achyuta, disciple of Śrī Madhusūdanāśrama.
